name: Molecular Biology
description:
Explore the **molecules of life** — DNA, RNA, and the processes that make cells work.

This quiz covers:
- Structure of DNA and RNA
- Nucleotides and base pairing
- Gene expression (transcription and translation)

> No prior biology degree required — just curiosity!

questions.text: What molecule carries **genetic information** in most living organisms?
questions.explanation:
**DNA** (Deoxyribonucleic acid) stores the genetic blueprint in most living organisms.

- It is found mainly in the **cell nucleus**
- It is a **double-stranded helix**
- It encodes instructions for making proteins

RNA also carries genetic information, but in most life it is an intermediate — a copy made from DNA. Some viruses (like HIV) use RNA as their primary genetic material.

questions.text: In a DNA double helix, what is the complementary base that pairs with **Adenine (A)**?
questions.explanation:
DNA base pairing follows strict rules:

| Base | Pairs with | Bonds |
|---|---|---|
| Adenine (A) | Thymine (T) | 2 hydrogen bonds |
| Guanine (G) | Cytosine (C) | 3 hydrogen bonds |

A useful mnemonic: **AT**omS and **GC** (think of a GC music label).

**Note:** In RNA, Uracil (U) replaces Thymine (T), so in RNA: A pairs with U.

questions.text: Which of the following are **components of a nucleotide**? Select all that apply.

choices.text: A nitrogenous base (A, T, G, or C)
An amino acid residue from the polypeptide chain

questions.explanation:
A **nucleotide** has three parts:

1. **Phosphate group** — provides the backbone's negative charge
2. **Sugar** — deoxyribose in DNA, ribose in RNA
3. **Nitrogenous base** — A, T, G, C (or U in RNA) — carries the genetic code

**Amino acids** are the building blocks of *proteins*, not nucleotides.

questions.text:
Which process uses **DNA as a template** to produce an RNA molecule?

```mermaid
graph LR
    DNA["🧬 DNA"] -->|"?"| RNA["📜 RNA"]
    RNA -->|"Translation"| Protein["🔬 Protein"]
```
questions.explanation:
The **Central Dogma** of molecular biology describes the flow of genetic information:

```mermaid
graph LR
    DNA["🧬 DNA"] -->|"Transcription"| RNA["📜 mRNA"]
    RNA -->|"Translation"| Protein["🔬 Protein"]
```

- **Transcription** — DNA → RNA (happens in the nucleus)
- **Translation** — RNA → Protein (happens at ribosomes)
- **Replication** — DNA → DNA (copying DNA before cell division)

questions.text: What is the difference between **DNA** and **RNA**? Describe at least two differences in your own words.
questions.explanation:
Key differences between DNA and RNA:

| Feature | DNA | RNA |
|---|---|---|
| Strands | Double-stranded | Single-stranded |
| Sugar | Deoxyribose | Ribose |
| Bases | A, T, G, C | A, **U**, G, C |
| Location | Mainly nucleus | Nucleus & cytoplasm |
| Role | Stores genetic info | Carries/reads genetic info |

**Summary:** DNA is the permanent blueprint stored in the nucleus. RNA is a temporary working copy used to build proteins.
